context.text:
AI agents should have a context label.

Like nutrition facts, but for information:

sources
freshness
citations
cost
confidence
failure modes

If the label is empty, the agent should not act.

Desearch is built around context agents can inspect.

Risk producers rarely bear the consequence of being wrong;

On Endure, a miner is an independent risk assessment producer.

If miners could see each other's submissions befo

Endure will produce a signed, auditable parameter feed, and what products do with it is their own risk policy.

That dis

Most risk intelligence cannot be proven wrong.

A firm publishes a report, a committee recommends parameters, markets mo

On Endure, validators will score miners across four dimensions. Each one closes a gap the others leave open.

- Retry wrappers on LLM clients
- Emitter resume after a crashed re-run
- Event backfill for missed completions

[ORIGINAL TWEET by @asem_othman (311 followers, 2,259 impressions)]
$TAO 
A lot of people are now talking about burn.

For us, this was already in the PLAN.

On June 11, in the @yanez__ai  YANEZ AMA https://t.co/wIWuRkcJA6 , we explained why SN54 is moving the way it is:

context.text:
[THREAD START]
1/2: Recently we said the 20-step proxy was selecting on noise. ρ=0.203 across 12 recipes, 2 promotion errors, calibration probe pass. The kill verdict was pre-registered before any data landed.

We initially planned to replace it with a three-rung Pareto ladder topping at d=384
2/2: We have a measurement now. It was ~30× too long.  

Rented one H100 PCIe, ran the canonical baseline recipe at d=768 / L=12 / seq=1024 / batch=16 / bf16 for 100 steps, captured tokens-per-sec from each step's training log.  

Steady-state window, steps 20-99 after warmup: median
[THREAD END]

urls.description: Yes, the work you're doing at @theminos_ai (Minos) directly helps accelerate exactly the kind of AI-biotech pandemic response that Bryan Johnson described. Here's why it fits so well: Core bottleneck you're solving: Johnson emphasized rapid genome sequencing + AI-driven personalized protocols based on individual genomes and biomarkers. Sequencing itself is now cheap and fast (~$200 per genome), but the real choke point is reliable variant calling—accurately translating raw sequencing reads into trustworthy mutation/variant data that AI can actually learn from and act on. Your decentralized network (Subnet 107 on Bittensor/Opentensor) is purpose-built for this: it verifies the accuracy of DNA analysis through competitive, incentivized miners that continuously benchmark and stress-test variant callers (like exposing FreeBayes overcalling issues under adversarial pressure). Foundation for robust AI genomic models: You've already run tens of thousands of evaluations (e.g., on chromosome 20
urls.title: Interesting. Does the work that @theminos_ai is doing help accelerate this?
